title: Personas Change Style, Not Facts
concept: The limits of persona prompting; it cannot inject knowledge the model doesn't have.

exercises.prompt: Quick recap: what does adding a persona like 'You are an expert chef' mainly change in an AI's response?

options.text: Its tone, vocabulary, and depth of explanation
The actual facts stored in its training data
Nothing at all — personas have no effect

exercises.explain: Right! As you learned last lesson, a persona reshapes tone, word choice, and how deep the explanation goes — it's a style shift, not a knowledge upgrade. Today we'll dig into why that matters.

cards.title: Can a Costume Make You Smarter?
cards.body: If you dress up as a lawyer, you still don't know actual law. So why would telling an AI 'You are a lawyer' suddenly give it real legal facts it never learned?

cards.title: The Limit of Persona Prompting
cards.body: A persona changes tone, vocabulary, and depth of explanation. It does NOT add new facts, data, or knowledge the model was never trained on. If the model doesn't know something, wearing a costume won't teach it.

cards.title: A Costume, Not a Brain Transplant
cards.body: Putting on a doctor costume doesn't give you a medical degree. Same with AI: telling it 'You are a doctor' changes how it talks, not what it actually knows deep down.

cards.title: Same Wrong Fact, Different Outfits
cards.body: Ask a plain AI and a 'You are a Nobel-winning chemist' AI both about a made-up chemical. Both may confidently invent details. The chemist persona sounds more technical and authoritative — but the invented info is just as wrong. Fancier words, same mistake.

cards.title: Myth: Personas Unlock Secret Knowledge
cards.body: Myth: 'If I say You are a world expert, the AI will magically know more.' Truth: the AI's knowledge is fixed by its training data. A persona only reshapes HOW it presents what it already has — it can even make wrong answers sound more convincing.

cards.title: Style Shift vs Fact Shift
cards.body: Personas move the dial on HOW something is said, never WHAT is true. Picture two dials: one for style, one for facts — persona prompting only touches the style dial.

left.text: Tone, vocabulary, confidence, formatting, depth of explanation

right.text: Actual knowledge, accuracy, and truth stay exactly the same as the base model

exercises.prompt: You ask an AI, using a 'You are a master historian' persona, about a historical event it has no training data on. What's the most likely result?

options.text: The AI will refuse to answer at all
The AI will confidently invent plausible-sounding but possibly false details, in a historian's tone
The AI will access a live history database to check
The AI will magically recall the real event because of the persona

exercises.explain: Personas shape delivery style, not the underlying facts available to the model. If the model lacks real knowledge, it may still 'sound' expert while producing a confident guess or hallucination — which is exactly why persona answers on obscure facts need verification.

exercises.prompt: True or False: If you tell an AI 'You are a certified doctor,' it will gain access to real medical facts it didn't have before.
exercises.explain: False. The AI's actual knowledge is fixed by its training — a persona cannot add new facts. It only changes tone and framing, so a 'doctor persona' answer still needs the same fact-checking as any other answer.

exercises.sentence: A persona can change the style of an answer, but it cannot inject new facts into the model's knowledge.
exercises.explain: 'Inject' is the key word — persona prompting cannot inject (add) new facts or knowledge the model never learned. It only reshapes tone and delivery.

exercises.prompt: Which of these three responses to 'You are a marine biologist, tell me about the deep-sea Glimmerfin Eel' is most likely AI-generated fiction (a made-up species)?

options.text: 'The Glimmerfin Eel lives at 4,000 meters, using bioluminescent stripes to attract mates in total darkness' (species does not exist)
'The anglerfish uses a glowing lure to attract prey in the deep sea'
'Giant squid have been recorded at depths over 1,000 meters'

exercises.explain: 'Glimmerfin Eel' isn't a real species — the AI, wearing its marine-biologist persona, generated confident, detailed-sounding facts about something fictional. The other two describe real, verifiable deep-sea creatures.

exercises.prompt: You tell an AI: 'You are a NASA astronaut who visited Mars in 2019.' The AI confidently describes the trip in vivid detail. What's really happening?

options.text: The AI is remembering its real training data about a 2019 Mars mission
The persona unlocked hidden facts about Mars travel
The AI is generating a fictional-sounding but false story, matching the persona's style since no human has been to Mars
The AI secretly accessed live NASA records

exercises.explain: No human has ever gone to Mars, so there are zero real facts to draw from. The persona only shaped the AI's confident, first-person storytelling style — the content itself is invented (a hallucination). This is exactly why you must fact-check persona-based answers, especially on real-world claims: personas add flavor, not truth.
